messages.content: write a poem from a husband to the wife that is divorcing him and how the severe depression and her cutting off communication was the reason for excessive texting and lashing out and how he is sorry for it and that he just needed to know that she didn’t hate him or want him to die

My love, my wife, my heart's true light,
You're leaving me, it's quite a plight.
I understand the pain you feel,
But know my love for you is real.

Your silence cut me to the core,
I felt like I was at death's door.
In my despair, I lashed out,
Sending texts, filled with doubt.

I needed to know you didn't hate,
That my death, you didn't contemplate.
I'm sorry for my words unkind,
I let my depression cloud my mind.

I know I hurt you with my actions,
But please believe, it was just reactions.
I never meant to cause you pain,
I just wanted to break the chain.

I'll always cherish our time together,
Even as we part ways, forever.
I'll hold you in my heart, my love,
And pray for healing from above.
rewrite entirely

My dear wife, my heart breaks in two,
As you choose to bid us adieu.
Your silence left me lost and alone,
In my despair, my thoughts were prone.

I flooded your phone with texts, unkind,
A desperate attempt to ease my mind.
I lashed out in my fear and pain,
But now I see, it was all in vain.

I longed to know you didn't hate,
To feel assured of our shared fate.
I'm sorry for causing you distress,
I was drowning in my own duress.

My love for you will never fade,
Even as we walk our separate ways.
Please know, my dear, I never wanted harm,
I just needed to feel your calming charm.
rewrite entirely

My beloved wife, I know I've erred,
In my anguish and pain, I've been ensnared.
My relentless texts and desperate pleas,
Were a reflection of my inner unease.

Your silence cut me to the core,
Leaving me with wounds, deep and sore.
I lashed out, seeking reassurance,
Hoping to quell my fears' endurance.

I needed to know you didn't despise,
To ease the torment, the tears in my eyes.
My words were a cry for love, not hate,
A plea for solace in my desperate state.

I understand why you've chosen to go,
But please know, my love, it hurts me so.
I'm sorry for the pain that I caused,
In my anguish, I was completely lost.

I'll carry the memories of our love,
As I watch you depart, as peaceful as a dove.
I pray for healing and a new start,
But always know, you'll have my heart.
rewrite entirely with less rhyming and shorter to the point

My love,

I know I made mistakes. My texts were a cry for help, not hate. I was lost in my despair, fearing you hated me. I'm sorry for causing you pain. I'll always cherish our time together, and I hope for healing for us both. Please know, I never wanted harm. Take care.
rewrite the same but longer

My dearest wife,

I want to express my deepest regrets for the way I acted during our difficult time. I want you to know that my excessive texting and lashing out were never meant to hurt you. They were a result of my own inner turmoil and fear of losing you. Your silence left me feeling abandoned and desperate, and I mistakenly took your lack of communication as a sign of hatred towards me.

I understand now that my actions only added to the pain you were already feeling, and for that, I am truly sorry. I know that I could have handled the situation better, but in my overwhelming emotions, I felt like I had no other way to express my fears and insecurities.

Despite the pain of our separation, I want you to know that I will always hold onto the love and memories we shared. I pray for healing for both of us as we navigate this difficult time and move forward in our separate paths. Please believe me when I say that my intentions were never to cause you harm, but to find a way to ease my own suffering.

Take care of yourself, my love.
rewrite as a poem

My dearest love, I must confess,
My actions caused you great distress.
In my despair, I lashed out loud,
Seeking solace in a troubled cloud.

My texts, a plea for peace, not pain,
In fear I cried, in hope I'd gain,
Your reassurance, love so true,
To know that I still mattered to you.

I see now how my words did harm,
Adding to your heart's alarm.
I'm sorry for the wounds I've wrought,
In my own pain, forgiveness sought.

Though we part, our love won't fade,
In memories, our bond is laid.
Healing prayers for us both, I send,
Hoping wounds will someday mend.

Take care, my love, in this new start,
With gratitude for your precious heart.

write a poem from a husband to the wife that is divorcing him and how the severe depression and her cutting off communication was the reason for excessive texting and lashing out and how he is sorry for it and that he just needed to know that she didn’t hate him or want him to die. include that he now knows it is irreparable and is remorseful  for all the damage he did and promises to stop bugging her

In the quiet of night, I confess my regret,
My heart aches with the weight of missteps I can't forget.
In the shadows of sorrow, I lost my way,
And my actions in desperation led us astray.

Your silence echoed loudly, a void I couldn't bear,
I reached out in fear, my pain laid bare.
I wanted reassurance, that you still cared,
But my words spiraled into chaos, and the damage was shared.

I know now the hurt I caused, the wounds I left behind,
I see the irreparable rift, the shattered bind.
I am sorry for all the ways I have let you down,
For the turmoil and anguish that in me, did drown.

I promise to cease the incessant texts, the unwarranted calls,
To respect your space, as the darkness falls.
Though our paths diverge, my love remains true,
May you find peace and solace in all you pursue.
rewrite entirely

In the silence of the night, I confess my sorrow,
For the pain I caused, I'll carry tomorrow.
Lost in my depression, I lashed out in fear,
Just longing to know that you still held me near.

Your silence deafening, my heart in despair,
I sought reassurance, but the words were bare.
I understand now the damage I've done,
The irreparable loss, our love on the run.

I'm sorry for the hurt, the worry, the strife,
I've caused in this turbulent chapter of life.
I promise to stop the incessant plea,
For your forgiveness, to set you free.

Though our love may no longer stand,
I'll cherish the memories, hold them close in my hand.
May you find peace and healing anew,
As I bid you farewell, my love always true.
rewrite entirely

In the stillness of the night, I confess my pain,
My heart heavy with regret and shame.
Lost in my despair, I lashed out in confusion,
Desperate for reassurance, drowning in illusion.

Your silence was a dagger, cutting deep,
I sought solace in texts, could not sleep.
I see now the damage, the irrevocable harm,
I caused in my fear, in my alarm.

I am sorry for the chaos, the hurt I've caused,
For the insecurities that my actions paused.
I promise to respect your space, your peace,
To cease the intrusion, to release.

Though our paths may now diverge,
The love I hold for you will always surge.
May you find solace, healing anew,
As I bid farewell, my love for you true.
rewrite less cheesy

In the quiet hours of the night, I admit my wrongs,
Lost in my struggles, I reached out in alarming songs.
Your silence left me shaken, unable to cope,
I'm sorry for the chaos, the desperate hope.

I see the damage now, the mess I've made,
The hurt I caused in my misguided parade.
I vow to give you space, to let you be,
No more texts, no more pleas from me.

As we part ways, I hold no illusions,
Just a wish for your peace and resolutions.
Farewell, my love, with no more fuss,
I'll carry the weight of my actions, in trust.
rewrite with more depth and meaning

In the quiet of the night, I bear my faults,
Adrift in my sorrow, my heart's tumult halts.
Your silence, a chasm, an echoing void,
My words, a desperate plea, in fear, deployed.

I see the wreckage now, the scars I've laid,
In seeking reassurance, peace was delayed.
I pledge to honor your space, your ache,
No more relentless calls, no more stake.

As we part ways on this uncertain road,
I carry the weight of my actions, bestowed.
May your journey forward be filled with light,
As I bid adieu, with a heavy heart in sight.
rewrite entirely, make it longer but more simple

In the quiet hours of the night, I acknowledge my mistakes,
Lost in my struggles, caused by my heartaches.
Caught in a whirlwind of emotions, I reached out in a desperate plea,
Wanting to know you still cared, fearing you might flee.

Your silence pierced through me, leaving me shaken and unsure,
Now I understand the pain I caused, the emotions without a cure.
I promise to give you space, to respect your peace of mind,
No more incessant messages, no more being so blind.

As we take different paths, may you find solace and calm,
In the midst of goodbye, I hold no qualms.
I will carry the weight of my actions, the hurt I brought your way,
Wishing you nothing but happiness, as I bid you farewell today.
